compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G3 container coverage audit (parent WS-EVAL-EXISTING vs this child c156 + full sibling roster): clean — no findings.

4-bucket audit of parent's 5 acceptance criteria:
- AC1 (3 packaged eval specs, validate_eval_spec-clean, disciplined gates:false cross-family judges, at_least(k)/epochs, coverage threshold, non-empty gold_set): FULLY covered by this child c156 (authors all 3 specs with epochs>=2, at_least(k), coverage threshold, GPT-4o cross-family judge gates:false, non-empty gold_set).
- AC2 (deterministic-scorer registry; every name -> pure fn; validate_scorer rejects unregistered): STRUCTURAL for c156 (it consumes registered scorers); covered by sibling 476f.
- AC3 (run_eval() runs live via Inspect; 3 agentic reviewers via context-injection solver + stubbed/fixture repo tools): STRUCTURAL for c156; covered by siblings 4569 (run_eval/Inspect) + 7402 (solver + tool stubbing).
- AC4 (eval-discipline CI gates all 7 specs at zero API cost; offline tests assert dataset balance + non-empty gold_set): FULLY covered by this child c156 (offline unit tests assert validity/gold_set/balance/registered scorers + eval-discipline CI validates all 7 specs at zero cost); schema/shape-validation half supplied by 476f.
- AC5 (eval-live workflow_dispatch+weekly, non-blocking, cached, concurrency-capped, fork-safe, spend-cap docs): STRUCTURAL for c156; covered by sibling 4569.

Uncovered-criterion sweep against the WHOLE roster: none. Every parent AC is covered by the union of children (AC1->c156, AC2->476f, AC3->4569+7402, AC4->c156/476f, AC5->4569). This child's own ACs map cleanly onto parent AC1+AC4 / Phase 1 scope with no orphan scope.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
G4 child-consistency audit (parent WS-EVAL-EXISTING vs this child c156 + full sibling roster: 476f, 7402, 4569). 7 cross-child interaction modes checked under high-confidence / benign-reading filter — clean, no findings.

- Implicit shared state: c156 consumes the dataset-case schema + deterministic-scorer registry from 476f (depends_on 476f, explicit) and shares the validation/CI substrate (validate_eval_spec/validate_scorer, eval.yml) with 476f (schema/shape checks) and 4569 (eval-live job). All surfaced via deps or distinct CI jobs, not hidden state. Consistent.
- Conflicting assumptions: c156 sets generator anthropic:claude-opus-4-8, epochs>=2, at_least(k), coverage threshold, GPT-4o cross-family judge gates:false; 4569 consumes these generically and treats the judge as report-only ("llm-judge reports"), consistent with gates:false. c156's "scorers all registered (foundation task)" matches 476f's registry contract. eval-discipline described identically (validates all 7, zero API cost) by c156 AC5 and 4569 AC5. No contradiction.
- Dependency gap: c156 AC2 (scorers all registered) + AC5 (eval-discipline validates via the registry-backed gate) require 476f's registry + validate_scorer extension — depends_on 476f present. No missing upstream.
- Scope overlap: c156's eval-discipline VALIDATION job vs 4569's eval-live job both touch .github/workflows/eval.yml but are partitioned into distinct jobs (discipline vs live) and reference eval-discipline consistently (required, zero-cost). gold_set seeding (c156) vs label adjudication (7402): NOT a high-confidence overlap — the established pattern (src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/README.plan-review.md:36-42; plan-review-finder.eval.yaml:179-189) explicitly ships the inline gold_set as best-effort SEED labels "pending human adjudication," with adjudication an explicitly-deferred ongoing post-implementation concern. c156 ships the inline seed gold_set (its spec-authoring job); 7402 owns the adjudication PROCESS + provisional marking "per the existing eval README convention." This is a convention-blessed partition (seed-now / adjudicate-later), so the benign-reading filter applies and I do NOT raise it.
- Ordering violation: deps enforce foundation(476f) -> specs(c156) -> wiring(4569, depends_on c156). c156 shipping its seed gold_set before 7402's adjudication is the INTENDED order per the README ("seed pending adjudication"; 7402's adjudication revises the seed), not an inversion. No forced c156<->7402 link is required because c156 AC1 (non-empty gold_set) is satisfiable by the provisional seed alone. Clean.
- Consumer impact: c156's 3 specs (dataset/gold_set/at_least(k)/epochs/coverage/judge config) are consumed by 4569's Inspect Task assembly + kappa-over-gold_set step; 4569 depends_on c156. Consistent.
- Residual references: c156 references the plan-review/code-quality spec pattern, the docs/experiments FP taxonomy, and the eval README convention — all pre-existing infra confirmed present (eval_specs/*.eval.yaml, README.plan-review.md). No dangling references to removed/renamed siblings.

This child's ACs (3 validate_eval_spec-clean specs; registered scorers + disciplined gates:false judge; balanced corpus-tagged seeded datasets; offline tests for validity/gold_set/balance/registered scorers; eval-discipline validates all 7 at zero cost) map cleanly onto parent AC1 + AC4 (Phase 1) and stay consistent with sibling-owned AC2 (476f) / AC3 (4569+7402) / AC5 (4569) deliverables. No findings.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
Implemented (T2 / Phase 1):
- 3 packaged eval specs added under src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/: completion-verifier (fail/pass; emits_valid_verdict + recall_on_incomplete + no_false_fail_on_complete; judge finding_cites_criterion_and_is_grounded), ticket-quality (finding/pass; emits_valid_review_result + recall_on_seeded_defects + no_fire_on_good_cases; judge finding_is_useful_and_actionable), spec-alignment (finding/pass, BATCH unit spec+epics; recall_on_gaps_and_conflicts + no_fire_on_aligned; judge gap_is_real_and_actionable).
- Each: generator anthropic:claude-opus-4-8, epochs=3, at_least(2), coverage>=0.8, balanced corpus-tagged datasets (rebar/snap/dso), non-empty gold_set. Repo-state hints live in a 'note' metadata field, NOT in the fed ticket_context/spec (no answer leakage).
- All 3 pass validate_eval_spec(strict=True); all deterministic scorers are registered (T1); judges are cross-family pinned + gates:false.
- tests/unit/test_new_eval_specs.py (9 tests): strict-clean, registered scorers, disciplined judges, balanced datasets, non-empty gold. ruff+mypy clean; eval suites green; 'rebar prompt eval completion-verifier' resolves.
Advisory triage (plan-review): the E2 'claude-opus-4-8 is a typo' finding is INVALID — Opus 4.8 is a real model id and matches every existing spec's generator convention. Hardcoded thresholds/graceful-degradation/offline-only-proof advisories map to the live-harness task (T4); datasets are seed labels pending human adjudication per the existing eval README convention.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
## Completion Verification — PASS

Verified every acceptance criterion against the repository. All 5 ACs are met.

---

### AC1 — 3 specs exist, each `validate_eval_spec`-clean with required shape

**MET.**

All 3 files present under `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/`:
- `completion-verifier.eval.yaml`
- `ticket-quality.eval.yaml`
- `spec-alignment.eval.yaml`

Each has:
- `model: anthropic:claude-opus-4-8` (`completion-verifier.eval.yaml:9`, `ticket-quality.eval.yaml:8`, `spec-alignment.eval.yaml:9`)
- `epochs: 3` (all three specs, confirmed)
- `gate: at_least(2)` (all three)
- `coverage_threshold: 0.8` (all three)
- Non-empty `gold_set` (4 entries each)

`test_new_spec_loads_and_is_strict_clean` in `tests/unit/test_new_eval_specs.py:17–20` parametrically calls `validate_eval_spec(spec, strict=True)` and asserts `[]` for all 3.

---

### AC2 — Deterministic scorers all registered; judge is disciplined + `gates: false`

**MET.**

All deterministic scorer names used across the 3 specs are present in `REGISTRY` in `src/rebar/llm/eval_scorers.py:203–224`:

| Spec | Scorer names |
|---|---|
| completion-verifier | `emits_valid_verdict`, `recall_on_incomplete`, `no_false_fail_on_complete` |
| ticket-quality | `emits_valid_review_result`, `recall_on_seeded_defects`, `no_fire_on_good_cases` |
| spec-alignment | `emits_valid_review_result`, `recall_on_gaps_and_conflicts`, `no_fire_on_aligned` |

Each spec's single `llm-judge` scorer uses `openai:gpt-4o-2024-08-06` (cross-family to `anthropic:`), `temperature: 0`, `seed: 7`, `snapshot: "2024-08-06"`, and `gates: false` (confirmed in all three YAML files).

`test_new_spec_scorers_are_registered_and_disciplined` in `tests/unit/test_new_eval_specs.py:23–35` checks all three invariants parametrically.

---

### AC3 — Datasets balanced, corpus-tagged, seeded from real cases

**MET.**

Every dataset case in all 3 specs carries a `corpus:` tag (search across `eval_specs/` returned `corpus:` hits for every case). Balances:
- `completion-verifier`: 3 × `expect: fail` + 3 × `expect: pass`
- `ticket-quality`: 3 × `expect: finding` + 3 × `expect: pass`
- `spec-alignment`: 3 × `expect: finding` + 3 × `expect: pass`

Fixture-repo state is carried in a `note:` metadata field, not injected into `ticket_context`/`spec` fields (no answer leakage). Cases are drawn from rebar/snap/dso corpora with recognizable real-world patterns (coverage gaps, dependency cycles, contradictory specs, etc.).

---

### AC4 — Offline unit tests assert per-spec: validity, non-empty gold_set, dataset balance, registered scorers

**MET.**

`tests/unit/test_new_eval_specs.py` (45 lines, 9 parametrized test cases = 3 specs × 3 test functions):
- `test_new_spec_loads_and_is_strict_clean` — validity via `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)`
- `test_new_spec_scorers_are_registered_and_disciplined` — registered names + `gates: false` + `validate_scorer`
- `test_new_spec_dataset_balanced_and_gold_present` — non-empty `gold_set` + FIRE/NOFIRE balance check

All 4 per-spec dimensions covered.

---

### AC5 — `eval-discipline` CI validates all 7 specs at zero API cost

**MET.**

The `eval-discipline` job in `.github/workflows/eval.yml:26–53` includes a "Validate every packaged eval spec" step (lines 41–53) that uses:
```python
specs = list(Path("src/rebar/llm/eval_specs").glob("*.eval.yaml"))
for p in specs:
    errs = ev.validate_eval_spec(yaml.safe_load(p.read_text()))
    assert not errs
```
This glob picks up all 7 specs (the pre-existing 4 plus the new 3) at zero API cost. The per-spec offline unit tests (`test_new_eval_specs.py`) run under the main `test.yml` workflow (`pytest -m "not integration and not external"`).

---

**Verdict: PASS** — all 5 acceptance criteria are demonstrably satisfied by the implementation.

compiled_state.description:
# Author the 3 eval specs + offline discipline tests (Phase 1)

## What

Author packaged specs mirroring the plan-review/code-quality pattern, using the
schema + registry from the foundation task:
- `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/completion-verifier.eval.yaml`
- `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/ticket-quality.eval.yaml`
- `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/spec-alignment.eval.yaml`

Each: generator `anthropic:claude-opus-4-8`, epochs>=2, `at_least(k)`, coverage
threshold, >=1 deterministic gating scorer (registered), a report-only cross-family
judge (GPT-4o, temp 0/seed/dated snapshot, gates:false), a balanced corpus-tagged
dataset (finding/pass[/fail]) seeded from real cases (docs/experiments FP taxonomy +
rebar's own tickets), and a non-empty gold_set.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] 3 specs exist under `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/`, each validate_eval_spec-clean.
- [ ] Each spec's deterministic scorers are all registered (foundation task); judge is disciplined + gates:false.
- [ ] Datasets are balanced, corpus-tagged, and seeded from real observed cases where available.
- [ ] Offline unit tests assert per-spec: validity, non-empty gold_set, dataset balance, and registered scorers.
- [ ] `eval-discipline` CI validates all 7 specs (the new 3 included) at zero API cost.

file_impact.reason: new eval spec for the completion-verifier reviewer
new eval spec for the ticket-quality reviewer
new eval spec for the spec-alignment reviewer (batch unit)
offline strict-validation tests for the 3 new specs

compiled_state.title: Author 3 eval specs + offline discipline tests (Phase 1)
